sitePermissionsPanel.title: Website Capabilities
sitePermissionsPanel.description: The extension declares normal website access at install time and uses static content scripts on http/https pages. Internal pages, extension pages, about:, and file: are not collectable by default.

hostAccess.title: Website Access
hostAccess.desc: This version no longer uses runtime website-access prompts. http://*/* and https://*/* are declared in the manifest, so normal website capabilities work immediately after install.
hostAccess.installNote: Access only covers normal http/https pages. Cookies are read only by name for local rule matching; raw values are never stored, shown, or sent to AI.

technologyStack.title: Technology-stack detection
technologyStack.desc: A plugin reads public signals such as headers, meta tags, script URLs, DOM, JS globals, request URLs, and cookie names, then exposes a compact chat-top entry.
technologyStack.privacy: When automatic context is on, AI receives only a technology-stack summary, never raw HTML, cookie values, script snippets, or long CSS.

pageTools.title: Enable page tools
pageTools.desc: Controls in-page overlay tools (Selection Assistant / Element Picker). When disabled, these tools will be unavailable.
pageTools.disabledHint: Page tools are disabled in settings

browserContext.title: Automatic browser context
browserContext.desc: Controls automatic browser-context collection for send, resend, regenerate, and manual refresh. When disabled, no page body will be fetched automatically.
browserContext.scopeHint: This switch only affects automatic browser-context collection. Selection Assistant and Element Picker are still controlled by Page Tools.
browserContext.fullPagePromptCharsHint: Only affects the top-bar full-page mode toggle. The default body budget is 24,000 chars.
browserContext.tagRulesDesc: Assign a default profile to assistant tags. When multiple tags match, only the highest-priority rule wins.
browserContext.emptyRules: No tag rules yet. Assistants fall back to Lightweight Page when no rule matches.
browserContext.singleWinnerHint: Rules use a single-winner model: assistant overrides always win over tag rules, and tag rules only resolve by highest priority.
